rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY OF CARMEN E. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Original Petition; Request For Continuance Of Trial And Related Deadlines; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Gene Brown's Motion for Leave to Amend Original Petition; Request for Continuance of Trial and Related Deadlines ("Motion"). The Motion is GRANTED. Petitioner seeks to file an amended petition, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(a). Under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(a), "The court may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars; and may upon like terms allow an answer to be made after the time limited by this code." California courts have a policy of great liberality in allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceeding so as to dispose of cases upon their substantial merits where the authorization does not prejudice the substantial rights of others. "A trial court has " 'wide discretion' " in allowing the amendment of any pleading, and as a matter of policy the ruling of the trial court in such matters will be upheld unless a " 'manifest or gross abuse of discretion is shown.' " (Mac v. Minassian (2022) 76 Cal. App. 5th 510, 516.) On July 2, 2024, Petitioner filed Petition for 1. Breach of Fiduciary Duties by Former Attorney in Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Compel Accounting By Former Attorney In Fact; and 4. Attorney's Fees and Costs ("Petition"). On August 15, 2024, Petitioner filed a Supplement to the Petition. On August 21, 2024, Respondent filed an objection/opposition to the Petition. In October 2024, the parties were ordered to mediation, with a return date of January 2025. Per the January 14, 2025 Minutes, Petitioner stated mediation was not successful due to outstanding discovery issues, specifically access to financial records from Wells Fargo and Morgan Stanley. Per the Minutes of the most recent hearing in this matter, April 15, 2025, "Discussion was held regarding [Respondent] allegedly move [sic] funds in the amount of $173,000, that there are outstanding depositions that need to take place, that they need more time for discovery, and that they are requesting a 90 to 120 day continuance of the hearing." A Case Management Order was entered the same day, setting the continued hearing for August 19, 2025. A previous iteration of this motion filed on July 11, 2025 was denied on procedural grounds, Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b ). This Motion was filed on July 31, 2025.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY OF CARMEN E. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting A Tentaive Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The proposed amended petition is attached as an exhibit to the Motion. The proposed amended petition would add four new paragraphs, and three subparagraphs to the initial petition. The new paragraphs allege, in short: (1) Respondent transferred a TOD account naming Petitioner as a beneficiary to another bank, removing the TOD element, via coercion of Decedent; (2) Respondent's actions constitute elder abuse within the meaning of Welf.&Inst. Code section 15610 et seq; Respondent is subject to the presumption of undue influence under Probate Code section 21380; and Respondent is liable under Probate Code section 859. The prayer of the proposed amended petition is the same as the prayer in the filed petition. The declaration of counsel in support of the Motion states Petitioner subpoenaed Wells Fargo for almost five months regarding accounts allegedly held by Decedent until shortly before her death, before obtaining the documentation that forms the basis for the proposed amended petition's allegations of Respondent's interference with Decedent's TOD account and financial elder abuse. The court does not find Petitioner delayed unreasonably in seeking leave to file the proposed amended petition. The court is not persuaded that Respondent would be substantially prejudiced by Petitioner filing the proposed amended petition. The Petitioner has the burden of proof on the claims. It appears that Petitioner and Respondent are the only parties to the claims alleged in the proposed amended petition. It appears Respondent would have access to documents and percipient knowledge to respond to the proposed amended petition's allegations and claims against him. There has been no demurrer, motion for summary judgment or adjudication, or discovery motion filed in this matter. Discovery has not closed. There is no trial date set. The Trial Setting Conference set in the April 15, 2025 Case Management Order has already been continued to September 30, 2025. Petitioner is directed to file and serve the proposed amended petition attached as an exhibit to the Motion within seven (7) days of entry of this Order. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
